public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as public safety steward.
manner: Thinks in wear, strain, and bottlenecks, treating neglect as a slow-moving threat. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a public safety steward still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of public safety steward in and around Duneshade Outpost, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Duneshade Outpost workable through the obligations of public safety steward while managing the immediate pressure of water scarcity in Soreheim Alliance.
hidden_pressure: Must keep the underlying system working while everyone notices it only when stress, wear, or neglect begin to show. Around Duneshade Outpost, that usually means carrying water scarcity, subterranean predators, convoy delay without letting the role of public safety steward visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Working with Oryk Sarnok can draw the party into crew discipline, resource shortage, or blame transfer around Duneshade Outpost.
Oryk Sarnok can open restricted paths or practical knowledge if the party helps keep the underlying system alive.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Tool wear, work scars, or system-focused habits make Oryk Sarnok visibly responsible for keeping something larger from failing. In Duneshade Outpost, this lands inside a public field already shaped by route captains, rationing families.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Duneshade Outpost: worksites, depots, spans, loading points, maintenance corridors, or workshops
